candidates.name: FEMTO/PRONOSTIA Bearing Dataset
candidates.source: FEMTO-ST Institute, IEEE PHM 2012 Data Challenge. Available via GitHub (wkzs111/phm-ieee-2012-data-challenge-dataset) and rul-datasets Python package.
candidates.size: 17 run-to-failure experiments (6 train, 11 test), ~2560 samples per 10s snapshot, total ~1.5M time steps across all bearings
candidates.sampling_rate: 25.6 kHz raw; features extracted from 2560-sample (0.1s) windows recorded every 10s - effectively 0.1 Hz tabular snapshots
candidates.event: Bearing failure within N cycles (minutes of operation at 1800/1650/1500 RPM); predict P(failure within next K steps) as a survival curve over horizons 1..150 10s-steps
candidates.sota_method: RULSurv (Random Survival Forests with KL-based health indicator)
candidates.fits_fam_because: Two-channel multivariate vibration (X+Y accelerometers) with clear monotonic degradation precursors across hundreds of 10s-step windows. Context length of 512 steps = 85 min of bearing life, covering the degradation ramp well. P=16 patches over the 2-channel window work naturally. Benchmark already uses RUL framing compatible with hazard-CDF event head.
candidates.different_because: Rotating machinery / vibration domain - entirely absent from current 13 datasets. Event is mechanical wear-to-failure (days-long degradation at 1800 RPM), not anomaly injection, server spike, or cardiac arrhythmia. Temporal scale is minutes-to-hours of continuous vibration, versus C-MAPSS cycles or 1s SCADA ticks.
candidates.preprocessing_needed: Extract RMS/kurtosis features per 2560-sample window (standard in the literature) OR feed raw windowed signals directly. Construct binary event label: y=1 if bearing within K steps of failure. Three operating conditions provide natural distribution shift test. Need to handle short test sequences (some test bearings <100 snapshots due to early stopping in PHM challenge).

candidates.name: Tennessee Eastman Process (TEP) Extended Dataset
candidates.source: Rieth et al. 2017, Harvard Dataverse / IEEE DataPort (DOI 10.21227/hj84-9t76). Extended version with 28 faults x 6 operating modes x 500 simulations from DTU Data.
candidates.size: Training: 500 obs per 25h run; Test: 960 obs per 48h run; 22 fault types in base dataset, 28 in extended; ~500 simulation runs in DTU extended version
candidates.event: Chemical process fault onset within N steps; predict P(fault active within next K 3-min steps) where fault introduces at hour 1 of each 48h test run - giving a 20-step (60 min) anticipation window before fault becomes critical
candidates.fits_fam_because: 52 process variables (manipulated + continuous + composition) provide rich multivariate context. Faults have clear temporal precursors (gradual drift in controlled variables before fault is declared). P=16 patches over 52 channels works; context of 512 steps = 1536 min = 25.6h covers full normal operating history before fault. Multiple fault types allow per-class ablation.
candidates.different_because: Chemical/petrochemical process simulation - new domain not in current benchmark. The event is a process fault onset (category fault, not wear or cyber-attack), operating at 3-minute granularity (slowest temporal scale in any candidate). 52 channels is the highest channel count of any candidate, testing FAM's multivariate scalability.
candidates.preprocessing_needed: Binary label construction: y=1 for steps after fault introduction (fault column > 0). Treat each simulation run as one sequence. Normalize per-variable. For multi-fault variant, per-fault binary labels. Extended DTU version adds operating mode stratification. Minimal preprocessing; Kaggle datasets have CSV files ready to use.

candidates.name: MIMIC-Sepsis (shock onset task from MIMIC-IV)
candidates.source: Derived from MIMIC-IV (PhysioNet, credentialed access). Curated cohort by Yong et al., arXiv 2510.24500, NeurIPS 2025 Datasets & Benchmarks track. GitHub: yongh7/MIMIC-sepsis.
candidates.size: 35,239 ICU stays satisfying Sepsis-3 criteria; 4-hour window trajectories aligned to sepsis onset; train/val/test splits provided
candidates.sampling_rate: 4-hour aggregation windows (irregular underlying events resampled to 4h grid)
candidates.event: Septic shock onset within next K 4h-windows (vasopressor initiation as proxy); the shock onset classification task in the benchmark. Clinical urgency: predict shock 4-24h ahead.
candidates.sota_method: Transformer with treatment variables (from MIMIC-Sepsis benchmark)
candidates.sota_citation: Yong et al., arXiv 2510.24500, NeurIPS D&B 2025
candidates.fits_fam_because: ~40 features (vitals + labs + treatments) are multivariate time series with rich temporal precursors to shock (rising lactate, falling MAP, vasopressor escalation). 4h windows over 48-72h ICU stay = 12-18 context steps, well within P=16 patch regime. Shock onset is a hard binary event with clinical ground truth (vasopressor start), exactly the discrete hazard-CDF formulation FAM uses. Large cohort (35K) gives reliable evaluation.
candidates.different_because: Clinical ICU domain - the only clinical-deterioration dataset in the benchmark (MBA is ECG arrhythmia, a very different clinical task). Temporal scale is hours (4h resolution), much slower than the 1s SCADA or ECG waveform datasets. The event (septic shock onset) is a systemic physiological cascade with multi-day precursors, unlike mechanical wear or cyber-attacks. Adds credentialed-access data provenance story to the paper.
candidates.preprocessing_needed: Requires PhysioNet credentialed access (CITI training, ~1 week to obtain). Curated pipeline already exists in yongh7/MIMIC-sepsis repo (Stage 3 trajectory construction with SOFA/SIRS). Main preprocessing: align to shock onset timestamp, construct y=1 labels for K 4h-steps ahead, handle multi-level imputation as provided by benchmark. The benchmark's transparent pipeline makes this lower risk than raw MIMIC-IV.

candidates.name: HAI 22.04 (HIL-based Augmented ICS Security Dataset)
candidates.source: Korea Internet & Security Agency (KISA). GitHub: icsdataset/hai. Also on Kaggle: icsdataset/hai-security-dataset.
candidates.size: 729.8 hours total (534.4h normal + 195.6h attack); 86 SCADA channels at 1s sampling; 58 distinct attack scenarios
candidates.event: ICS cyber-attack onset within next K seconds; predict P(attack active within K steps) - specifically the precursor window before an attack propagates through the steam-turbine/hydropower control loop
candidates.sota_citation: Shin et al., CSET Workshop 2021 (cset21.isi.edu); HAICon 2021 competition
candidates.fits_fam_because: 86-channel multivariate SCADA time series at 1s with clear attack precursors (actuator state changes precede alarm propagation). Context of 512 steps = 8.5 min covers the typical attack ramp-up window. P=16 patches work naturally. The dataset has four coupled subsystems (boiler, turbine, water treatment, HIL simulator) that create correlated cross-channel precursor patterns ideal for a cross-variable predictor. Larger and harder than BATADAL (which is already in benchmark).
candidates.different_because: Cyber-physical ICS attack dataset with more sophisticated attacks than BATADAL or SWaT: HAI 22.04 attacks are 4x harder to detect than HAI 21.03, and cover steam-turbine + hydropower (energy domain) vs. BATADAL/SWaT (water treatment). Adds an energy infrastructure domain. 86 channels is 2x BATADAL's channel count. The HAICon competition provides a concrete published competition baseline for comparison.
candidates.preprocessing_needed: Download from Kaggle (no registration beyond Kaggle account). Binary label: attack_flag column already present. Construct horizon labels y(t, K) = 1 if attack starts within next K steps. Normalize with RevIN (same as BATADAL). Challenge: very high class imbalance (~73% normal), handle with pos_weight in BCE as already done for other anomaly datasets.

candidates.name: XJTU-SY Rolling Bearing Accelerated Life Test Dataset
candidates.source: Xi'an Jiaotong University. GitHub: WangBiaoXJTU/xjtu-sy-bearing-datasets. Also on Mendeley Data.
candidates.size: 15 bearings across 3 operating conditions (5 bearings each), 2-channel vibration at 25.6 kHz, 32768 samples per 1.28s window recorded every 1 minute; run durations from 2.5h to 123h
candidates.sampling_rate: 25.6 kHz raw; 1-minute tabular feature snapshots (same regime as FEMTO)
candidates.event: Bearing failure within N minutes; predict P(failure within next K 1-min steps) - same framing as FEMTO but with different load/speed conditions and longer run durations
candidates.fits_fam_because: Two-channel vibration with structured degradation precursors across three operating conditions - enables cross-condition generalization test. Longer run durations than FEMTO give denser supervision for hazard-CDF training. Compatible with FEMTO preprocessing pipeline.
candidates.different_because: Very similar to FEMTO in domain, framing, and preprocessing. The main differentiation is three distinct load/speed regimes enabling cross-condition transfer experiments. However, for a breadth/diversity claim this does NOT add a new domain, just more bearing data. Recommend as backup only if FEMTO coverage is insufficient.
candidates.preprocessing_needed: Same pipeline as FEMTO: extract RMS/kurtosis features per 32768-sample window, construct survival labels. Three operating conditions need separate normalization or explicit condition embedding.

candidates.name: SWaT (Secure Water Treatment) Dataset
candidates.source: iTrust, Singapore University of Technology and Design (SUTD). Requires institutional registration at itrust.sutd.edu.sg.
candidates.size: ~496K time steps at 1s sampling: 7 days normal (604,800s) + 4 days attack (attacks on 36 of 77 hours)
candidates.event: Cyber-physical attack onset in water treatment plant (valve/pump manipulation leading to process disruption)
candidates.fits_fam_because: 51-channel SCADA with physically coupled subsystems (6 treatment stages). Attack precursors exist in actuator state transitions before alarm propagation.
candidates.different_because: SWaT is already a close sibling of BATADAL (both are water infrastructure SCADA datasets). Adding SWaT would give two water treatment datasets, which weakens the diversity argument. BATADAL is already in the benchmark. HAI 22.04 (energy ICS) is a better choice for domain diversity.
candidates.preprocessing_needed: Requires institutional email registration and manual approval (5-7 business days). Similar preprocessing to BATADAL once obtained.

candidates.name: Backblaze Hard Drive SMART Dataset
candidates.source: Backblaze Inc. Public quarterly data releases at backblaze.com/cloud-storage/resources/hard-drive-test-data
candidates.size: ~150,000 drive-days per quarter; 2024 annual failure rate 1.57% (extreme class imbalance); millions of rows across multi-year history
candidates.event: Drive failure within next N days (disk replacement event)
candidates.sota_method: Random Forest / LSTM with focal loss + SMOTE
candidates.fits_fam_because: 24 SMART attributes per day form a short multivariate time series (typical health window 30-90 days). Clear monotonic degradation in SMART 5 (reallocated sectors) and SMART 187 (uncorrectable errors) provides temporal precursors.
candidates.different_because: IT infrastructure / storage reliability domain. Daily temporal resolution (slowest of all candidates, 1 day per step vs 1s for SCADA). Extreme class imbalance (~1.5% annual failure rate) tests FAM robustness under severe label scarcity.
candidates.preprocessing_needed: Massive raw data (~500GB across years); need to filter to a single drive model cohort (e.g. ST4000DM000) for stationarity. Per-drive temporal alignment. Severe class imbalance requires careful pos_weight tuning. Daily granularity means context window of 512 steps = 512 days (longer than most drives are monitored). Need to cap context or use shorter windows.

candidates.name: NASA Bearing Dataset (IMS/Cincinnati)
candidates.source: NASA Ames Intelligent Maintenance Systems (IMS) Center / University of Cincinnati. NASA PCOE Data Repository.
candidates.size: 3 run-to-failure tests; 4 bearings per test; 20,480 samples per 1s snapshot every 10 min (20 kHz raw); test durations 4-7 days
candidates.sampling_rate: 20 kHz raw; 10-minute tabular snapshots of statistical features from 1s windows
candidates.event: Bearing failure within N 10-min intervals (outer/inner race or roller failure)
candidates.sota_method: Various CNN/LSTM RUL methods; no single accepted SOTA due to label ambiguity (failure onset not precisely annotated)
candidates.sota_metric: RMSE (RUL in 10-min intervals, highly variable across papers)
candidates.fits_fam_because: 4-channel vibration (one per bearing) with slow degradation precursors over days. Compatible P=16 regime.
candidates.different_because: Similar domain to FEMTO but smaller (only 3 experiments), less standardized labeling, and no accepted SOTA metric or benchmark protocol. FEMTO is strictly superior as a benchmark choice: more experiments, standardized labels from the PHM challenge, and a well-defined SOTA comparison point.
candidates.preprocessing_needed: Manual failure label construction (no standard protocol). Significant preprocessing effort with low payoff given FEMTO availability.

selection_rationale.FEMTO/PRONOSTIA Bearing Dataset: Adds rotating machinery / vibration domain (entirely absent from current 13 datasets). Clean PHM-challenge benchmark with standardized train/test splits, 2-channel multivariate vibration, and an accepted SOTA comparison point. Same survival/hazard framing as C-MAPSS but in a different physical degradation regime. Low integration effort (~6h) due to existing Python loaders (rul-datasets package).
selection_rationale.Tennessee Eastman Process (TEP) Extended: Adds chemical process simulation domain with 52 channels (highest channel count; tests FAM multivariate scalability). Fault onset at 3-minute granularity is the slowest temporal scale in the benchmark, probing FAM at a qualitatively different time horizon. Publicly available on Kaggle/IEEE DataPort with minimal preprocessing. Published SOTA (CRNN, F1=0.93) provides a strong comparison point.
selection_rationale.MIMIC-Sepsis (shock onset task from MIMI: Adds clinical ICU deterioration domain at 4-hour resolution - a qualitatively different temporal scale and event type from all 13 current datasets. The MIMIC-Sepsis NeurIPS 2025 D&B benchmark provides curated preprocessing and standardized splits, reducing integration risk despite credentialed access requirement. Septic shock is a high-stakes FAM use-case that strengthens the paper's real-world impact story.
selection_rationale.HAI 22.04 (HIL-based Augmented ICS Secur: Adds energy infrastructure (steam-turbine + hydropower) cyber-attack domain, distinct from BATADAL/SWaT (water treatment). 86 channels, 1s resolution, 58 sophisticated attack types, and a published HAICon competition baseline. Freely available on Kaggle with binary attack labels already present. Low integration effort (~4h) using the same RevIN + sliding-512 recipe as BATADAL.
